programme.title: PL REWIND EP20: MCI v QPR 11/12
programme.sub-title: Manchester City v QPR on the final day of the 2011/12 season will forever be remembered as the most dramatic of finales to a Premier League season. Shaun Wright-Phillips and Joleon Lescott offer the perspective from both sides, with the final day drama at the Etihad set in the context of Manchester City’s Run-In and their pursuit of a first Premier League Title.
programme.desc: Manchester City v QPR on the final day of the 2011/12 season will forever be remembered as the most dramatic of finales to a Premier League season. Shaun Wright-Phillips and Joleon Lescott offer the perspective from both sides, with the final day drama at the Etihad set in the context of Manchester City’s Run-In and their pursuit of a first Premier League Title.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP08: (Subbed) SERGIO AGUERO HALL OF FAME SPECIAL
programme.sub-title: He’s fondly remembered for grabbing the last gasp goal that won Manchester City their first Premier League title back in 2012, and with 184 goals in just 275 appearances it clear to see why Sergio Aguero has been inducted into the hall of fame. We talk to him and his former teammates to reflect on an incredible career that saw him win 5 Premier League titles, 1 F.A. Cup and 6 League Cups.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP32: (Subbed) MANCHESTER CITY - THE SEASON THAT STARTED IT ALL
programme.sub-title: In a nail-biting climax to the 2011-2012 Premier League season, Manchester City clinched the title with unparalleled drama. Facing Queens Park Rangers on the final matchday, City secured a 3-2 victory with Sergio Agüero's iconic last-minute goal, securing their first league title in 44 years and etching that game into footballing folklore. They have since become the dominant power in English football and we look at how it all began.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP07: FERGIE v WENGER
programme.sub-title: Widely regarded as the two best managers in Premier League history, Sir Alex Ferguson and Arsene Wenger went toe to toe on the touchline on numerous occasions over the years. programme.title: Review of the Season 2024/2025 (LIV)
programme.sub-title: Relive another extraordinary Premier League campaign as Liverpool soared to an historic 20th league title, Nottingham Forest defied expectations and the promoted sides were strenuously challenged. Review of the Season documents all of the thrilling stories to emerge across the 380 matches.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP31: (Subbed) NOLBERTO SOLANO
programme.sub-title: We reflect on the life and career of Nolberto Solano, one of South America's football legends. From his early days in Peru to his pivotal role in the Premier League, we explore his journey, triumphs, and challenges. programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP42: CHAMPIONS SPECIAL WITH CURTIS JONES
programme.sub-title: With the remnants of the celebratory confetti barely gone from the Anfield pitch, Curtis Jones joins the Big Interview to reflect on an historic title-winning season. Having signed for his beloved team at just 9 years of age, the Liverpool born and bred midfielder explains just how much the title triumph means to him and sheds light on the impact of Arne Slot and the differences in approach of his new manager compared to Jurgen Klopp. In a year where Jones also enjoyed a goal-scoring senior debut for England and became a father for the first time, there's been plenty for him to celebrate.
programme.desc: With the remnants of the celebratory confetti barely gone from the Anfield pitch, Curtis Jones joins the Big Interview to reflect on an historic title-winning season. Having signed for his beloved team at just 9 years of age, the Liverpool born and bred midfielder explains just how much the title triumph means to him and sheds light on the impact of Arne Slot and the differences in approach of his new manager compared to Jurgen Klopp. In a year where Jones also enjoyed a goal-scoring senior debut for England and became a father for the first time, there's been plenty for him to celebrate.

programme.title: Review of the Season 2024/2025 (LIV)
programme.sub-title: Relive another extraordinary Premier League campaign as Liverpool soared to an historic 20th league title, Nottingham Forest defied expectations and the promoted sides were strenuously challenged. Review of the Season documents all of the thrilling stories to emerge across the 380 matches.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP34: (Subbed) CESC FABREGAS
programme.sub-title: A product of Barcelona’s famed La Masia youth academy, Cesc Fabregas is rightly remembered as one of the greatest midfield players of his generation. After making his Arsenal debut at just 16 years of age the diminutive Spaniard would go on to win titles in both England and Spain as well as becoming a World Cup winner in 2010. Now taking his first steps into coaching with Como 1907, Fabregas reflects on his footballing education as well as his hopes for the future in management.
programme.desc: A product of Barcelona’s famed La Masia youth academy, Cesc Fabregas is rightly remembered as one of the greatest midfield players of his generation. programme.sub-title: We catch up with Juan Mata in Australia, where the former Premier League star is now playing with Western Sydney Wanderers. The Spaniard reflects on his memorable years in England with Chelsea and Manchester United, sharing insights into his greatest moments, personal growth, and connection with fans. As he begins a new chapter down under, he also opens up about life after the Premier League and what lies ahead both on and off the pitch.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP34: (Subbed) CESC FABREGAS
programme.sub-title: A product of Barcelona’s famed La Masia youth academy, Cesc Fabregas is rightly remembered as one of the greatest midfield players of his generation. After making his Arsenal debut at just 16 years of age the diminutive Spaniard would go on to win titles in both England and Spain as well as becoming a World Cup winner in 2010. Now taking his first steps into coaching with Como 1907, Fabregas reflects on his footballing education as well as his hopes for the future in management.
programme.desc: A product of Barcelona’s famed La Masia youth academy, Cesc Fabregas is rightly remembered as one of the greatest midfield players of his generation. programme.title: PL STORIES EP01: (Subbed) JUAN MATA
programme.sub-title: We catch up with Juan Mata in Australia, where the former Premier League star is now playing with Western Sydney Wanderers. The Spaniard reflects on his memorable years in England with Chelsea and Manchester United, sharing insights into his greatest moments, personal growth, and connection with fans. As he begins a new chapter down under, he also opens up about life after the Premier League and what lies ahead both on and off the pitch.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP52: CHAMPIONS STORY
programme.sub-title: We dive into the fiercest rivalry in English football—Manchester United and Liverpool. As Liverpool draw level with United’s record haul of top-flight titles, this is a story of history, pride, and the relentless pursuit of greatness. With insight from icons on both sides—Ian Rush, Lou Macari, Brian Kidd, Michael Owen, Phil Thompson, and new Liverpool boss Arne Slot, we unpack the moments that have defined generations, split families, and lit up the Premier League. From legendary goals to seismic title races, this is the story of two giants locked in a battle for supremacy. Woven through it all is the significance of Liverpool’s record-equalling achievement—a milestone that adds a powerful new chapter to football’s greatest rivalry.
programme.desc: We dive into the fiercest rivalry in English football—Manchester United and Liverpool. As Liverpool draw level with United’s record haul of top-flight titles, this is a story of history, pride, and the relentless pursuit of greatness. With insight from icons on both sides—Ian Rush, Lou Macari, Brian Kidd, Michael Owen, Phil Thompson, and new Liverpool boss Arne Slot, we unpack the moments that have defined generations, split families, and lit up the Premier League. From legendary goals to seismic title races, this is the story of two giants locked in a battle for supremacy. Woven through it all is the significance of Liverpool’s record-equalling achievement—a milestone that adds a powerful new chapter to football’s greatest rivalry.

We meet the family, friends, and coaches who shaped his journey, offering a rare glimpse into the upbringing and mindset that forged a Premier League leader. Then, in Manchester, Rúben reflects on his career so far, his impact at City, and the values that continue to drive him on.
programme.desc: We chart the rise of Portuguese star Rúben Dias, from his early days in Portugal to the heart of Manchester City's defence. We meet the family, friends, and coaches who shaped his journey, offering a rare glimpse into the upbringing and mindset that forged a Premier League leader. Then, in Manchester, Rúben reflects on his career so far, his impact at City, and the values that continue to drive him on.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP03: PL GOALSCORERS
programme.sub-title: We celebrate the greatest goalscorers in Premier League history; the icons whose instinct, power, and flair have lit up stadiums and etched their names into footballing folklore. From Alan Shearer’s relentless consistency to Thierry Henry’s elegance, Sergio Agüero’s killer instinct to Harry Kane’s rise, we relive the moments that defined an era.
programme.desc: We celebrate the greatest goalscorers in Premier League history; the icons whose instinct, power, and flair have lit up stadiums and etched their names into footballing folklore. From Alan Shearer’s relentless consistency to Thierry Henry’s elegance, Sergio Agüero’s killer instinct to Harry Kane’s rise, we relive the moments that defined an era.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP14: (Subbed) NEMANJA VIDIC
programme.sub-title: Nemanja Vidić grew up in war-torn Serbia and went on to become one of the greatest defenders in English football. PL Stories sits down with him to explore his journey from Belgrade to Manchester. We also hear from former coaches and players about how his childhood shaped him into the person he is today and his lasting impact on the Premier League.
programme.desc: Nemanja Vidić grew up in war-torn Serbia and went on to become one of the greatest defenders in English football. We also hear from former coaches and players about how his childhood shaped him into the person he is today and his lasting impact on the Premier League.

programme.title: PL 100 ROBIN VAN PERSIE
programme.sub-title: Premier League 100, celebrates some of the leading names to have achieved iconic milestones within England’s top flight. From scoring over 100 goals, to making over 100 clean sheets, we speak to the legends that left their mark and reflect on the goals and saves that set them apart from their rivals

programme.title: MY SEASON GARETH BALE - 12/13
programme.sub-title: A look at a Premier League season through the eyes of Gareth Bale who excelled in 2012/2013. The highs and lows and everything in between, that made it a memorable and at times defining nine months for both Bale and Tottenham Hotspur

programme.title: PL STORIES EP27: (Subbed) CHICHARITO
programme.sub-title: Coming from a successful footballing family, Javier Hernandez was somewhat destined for football stardom. Chicharito talks us through his fairytale move to Manchester United as well as his return to Mexico and his hometown team, Club Deportivo Guadalajara.
